slides.title: Music as Universal Language
bullets: Prepare to read, discuss and write
slides.narration: Welcome to Grade 8 English, Unit 9: Music as Universal Language. Welcome to Unit 9, the final unit of Year 8. This unit is called "Music as Universal Language" because you will explore how music, performance, and the creative arts bring people together across cultures, languages, and backgrounds. Music is one of the most powerful forms of human expression. It can communicate emotions that words alone cannot capture. A song can make you feel joy, sorrow, excitement, or peace without you understanding a single word of the language in which it is sung. That is why we call music a universal language: it speaks to everyone. In this short lecture, preview the key ideas and prepare for the independent lesson.

slides.title: What you will achieve
bullets: Explain what it means to call music a universal language and discuss how the performing arts connect people across cultures
Read the story "The Curtain Never Lies" and answer comprehension and analysis questions using evidence from the text
Use thirty key vocabulary words related to performance, creativity, emotion, and integrity in your own speaking and writing
slides.narration: By the end of this unit, you will be able to explain what it means to call music a universal language and discuss how the performing arts connect people across cultures. you will also read the story "the curtain never lies" and answer comprehension and analysis questions using evidence from the text. you will also use thirty key vocabulary words related to performance, creativity, emotion, and integrity in your own speaking and writing. Keep these goals in mind as you work.

slides.title: Language for precise thinking
slides.narration: Your vocabulary work begins with these words: havoc, deft, render, muster, deploy, gait. Listen to each pronunciation in the vocabulary lab, study its meaning in context, and use the word accurately in your own discussion and writing.

slides.title: Read critically
bullets: Music as Universal Language: source text 1
Music as Universal Language: source text 2
slides.narration: The reading sequence includes Music as Universal Language: source text 1 and Music as Universal Language: source text 2. Read actively. Identify each text's central ideas, notice viewpoint and reliability, and support every inference with relevant evidence from the text.

slides.title: Present Perfect Continuous
bullets: What it means and why it matters The Present Perfect Continuous tense is used to talk about actions that started in the past and are still happening now,...
slides.narration: The first language focus is Present Perfect Continuous. What it means and why it matters The Present Perfect Continuous tense is used to talk about actions that started in the past and are still happening now, or that were happening recently and have just stopped. This tense emphasises the duration and ongoing nature of the action. It is one of the most useful tenses for describing recent experiences, long-running activities, and situations where you can see the visible result of an activity that has been in progress. For example, if your friend's... Notice the structure in the model examples, then apply it deliberately in connected sentences and paragraphs.

slides.title: Use English for a real purpose
bullets: Speaking 1: Role-Play Interview with a Performer
Explain, support and improve your ideas
slides.narration: In speaking, you will complete Speaking 1: Role-Play Interview with a Performer. Organise your ideas, use evidence and respond clearly to questions. In writing, you will plan, draft, check and revise. Use the success criteria before submitting your work.

slides.title: You are ready to begin
slides.narration: You are ready to begin. Start with the vocabulary lab, continue through reading and comprehension, apply the grammar focus, and complete the speaking and writing practices. Use AI English for hints and feedback, and bring your questions to the live sessions.
